Montauk the way
it used to be.

A family-owned country inn and oceanfront restaurant on the bluffs of Old Montauk Highway. Steps to the beach. A world away.

Reserve a Room Dinner & Drinks
Atlantic Ocean ViewsFrom every room
A Quiet TraditionAdults only · No pets
Since 1990 · Family Owned

A small inn with a long memory, set on the Atlantic in Montauk.

Surfside Inn & Restaurant has been family owned and operated for thirty-four years. Long before that, the building stood as the Surf & Sand and the Anzac House — and during World War II, served as billets for Coast Guard officers stationed along this stretch of coast.

The current owners have taken that history to heart. The Inn and Restaurant operate by a simple guiding line: Montauk the way it used to be.

34Years Family Run
1942Coast Guard Era
5 minWalk to Village
685 Old Montauk Hwy
“
Open windows lend you the beautiful sounds of the ocean, and a short stroll along the shoreline leads to Montauk Village.
— On Staying at Surfside
The Restaurant

Local seafood, prime steaks, and the Atlantic in every window.

The dining room is well known to locals and travelers alike — a place where the ocean sits just beyond the glass and the menu changes with what the boats bring in. Executive Chef Shawn Hewitt leads the kitchen, with freshly prepared local seafood, prime steaks, and a rotating list of house specialties.

In the warmer months, the outdoor patio opens up under striped umbrellas. Inside, dinner runs nightly at five, with happy hour at the bar from four-thirty.

Dinner5:00 PM nightly
Happy Hour4:30 – 6:00 PM
ClosedWednesdays
Reservations631.668.5958
Accommodations

Light, naturally decorated rooms — every one overlooking the Atlantic.

Oceanfront Suite

Spacious, air-conditioned, and bright — a private bath, ocean-facing windows that open to the sound of the surf, and a few yards to the beach path. The kind of room you came to Montauk to find.

Ocean View Private Bath A/C · TV · WiFi Refrigerator Coffeemaker
From $225Sun – Thu, May rates
Reserve

Adults only · Two guests per room · Two-night minimum · Stay three nights, Sunday through Thursday, your fourth is on us.

The Property

The bar, the deck, the path to the sand.

The Bar

Anchors, blue stools, an honest pour.

The Patio

Striped umbrellas at the bluff's edge.

The Dining Room

Skylights, white linens, the Atlantic.

A Note On The Sign

The crab on the gate has been here longer than most things in town.

Beach umbrellas, chairs, and towels are kept by the door. The path to the ocean is just beyond the front porch. A short walk down the shoreline — or along the paved path — and you're in Montauk Village.

It's a particular kind of quiet you find here. Different from the rest of the East End. Older.

— Montauk the way it used to be.
